title: Landmarks of the Deccan
description: The landmarks of the Deccan — the monuments, forts, palaces and tombs of the plateau, and the great institutions of learning, medicine and culture founded across the Deccan and Hyderabad State.
dedication: In memory of the Asaf Jahi dynasty
subtitle: The monuments, forts and palaces of the plateau, and the great institutions of learning and culture that shaped the Deccan and Hyderabad State.
body_html:
Beyond its dynasties and its sacred sites, the Deccan is marked by the works its rulers and citizens built to last — the forts, palaces, tombs and monuments that punctuate the plateau, and the institutions of learning, medicine and culture that carried the region into the modern age. Together they are the visible record of a civilisation.

This section gathers those landmarks in two strands: the monuments of stone and the institutions of mind — the universities, colleges, hospitals, libraries and museums founded across the Deccan and the old Hyderabad State.

Forts · Palaces · Tombs

Monuments

Golconda · Charminar · Gol Gumbaz · the tombs

The forts, palaces, gateways and tombs of the Deccan — Golconda and Daulatabad, the Charminar and Chowmahalla, the Gol Gumbaz of Bijapur and the Qutb Shahi necropolis — the monuments that define the skyline of the plateau.

Explore → Learning · Medicine · Culture

Institutions

Osmania · Jamia Nizamia · Salar Jung · the colleges

The great institutions of the Deccan and Hyderabad State — Osmania University, Jamia Nizamia, the Tibbi College, the Salar Jung Museum, the State Central Library and the schools and colleges that educated the region.

Explore →
